source: Varsity Tutors — provided directly by Nerdy (highest performing static ads)

ads.primary_text: May 2nd SAT. 8 weeks away. 2 sessions a week. 16 sessions total. That is about 200+ points.
ads.headline: Start This Week.
ads.audience: parents of high school students with upcoming SAT date
ads.notes: Zero adjectives. Pure specificity. Breaks the path to improvement into concrete countable steps — makes 200 points feel achievable not aspirational. Date-specific urgency. Most minimal ad in the set.

ads.primary_text: SAT Tutoring To Help Your Child Get Their SAT Score Up
ads.headline: SAT Tutoring To Help Your Child Get Their SAT Score Up
ads.audience: parents of high school students preparing for SAT
ads.notes: Visual does the heavy lifting — actual SAT score report showing 1170 to 1410 improvement. Headline is simple because the image already proves the outcome. Student holding both reports = relatable social proof.

ads.headline: 3.8 GPA But 1180 SAT?
ads.audience: parents of high-achieving students with disappointing SAT scores
ads.notes: Highest performing creative pattern. Creates instant tension between two real numbers the parent already knows. Implies 'your child is smart, the SAT is the problem' without saying it. Split panel visual shows confident student vs stressed student — before/after without time. Specific GPA and SAT score make it feel like they're talking directly to this parent.
brand_voice_patterns: reframe: child is smart, test is the problem

key_patterns_for_writer_agent: Use MATH AS URGENCY: break improvement into countable steps (8 weeks, 16 sessions, 200 points)
TENSION REFRAME: create conflict between two numbers the parent already knows (3.8 GPA vs 1180 SAT)
SPECIFIC SCORES ONLY: never say 'improved significantly', always say '1170 to 1410'
ZERO ADJECTIVES when possible: 'May 2nd SAT. 8 weeks away.' beats 'Your SAT is coming up soon'
THE CHILD IS NOT THE PROBLEM: always reframe — the test, the teaching style, the approach is the problem
DATE ANCHORING: specific dates create urgency that vague language cannot
